textBox.richText: QUICK LIST of CHANGES (see the CHANGES file in the distribution for a complete and detailed list) NEW FEATURES The main feature of this release is the availability of a graphical user interface for the compiler. This GUI, named CAPHY, is available in binary format for Windows (32 bits) and in source format for Unix-like systems. CAPHY allows - writing and editing CAPH source files (with syntax coloring) - grouping these files into "projects" - generating graph representation and viewing them (by running dotty) - running simulations (reading input files and writing output files) - generating SystemC code - generating VHDL code However, CAPHY does not allow compiling and running generated SystemC or VHDL code (this is too much dependent on the actual platform). The idea is that CAPHY is just a front-end used to generate code in a target directory and that these files are latter exploited by a separate tool or set of tools To get an idea of CAPHY you can have a look at part 2 of the tutorial.
